info.name: FAMA API Documentation
info.description: The Fama mobile API (v1) — token-authenticated endpoints for talents, brands and admins, sharing the same services and JSON envelope as the web app.

item.name: Talent authentication
item.description:
Token auth for the `talent` guard: public sign-up plus the shared
login/logout/refresh/me flow. Issued tokens carry the `talent` ability.

request.description:
Create a new talent account and return an ability-scoped token. The
profile starts unpublished; the public slug is generated by the model.

item.name: Authenticate credentials and issue an ability-scoped token.

request.description:
Throttled by the route middleware; credentials are verified against the
entity model (constant-time hash check) with a single generic error to
avoid leaking which half was wrong.

item.name: Return the authenticated entity (the token's tokenable).

Rotate the caller's token: revoke the one presented and issue a fresh
ability-scoped replacement. Sanctum tokens don't expire on their own, so
rotation is how a client renews a credential it wants to cycle.
Revoke the token presented with the request (single-device logout).
Brand authentication
item.description:
Token auth for the `brand` guard: public sign-up plus the shared
login/logout/refresh/me flow. Issued tokens carry the `brand` ability.

request.description:
Create a new brand account and return an ability-scoped token. The account
starts incomplete (onboarding gates the discovery feed) and unpublished.

item.name: Authenticate credentials and issue an ability-scoped token.

request.description:
Throttled by the route middleware; credentials are verified against the
entity model (constant-time hash check) with a single generic error to
avoid leaking which half was wrong.

item.name: Return the authenticated entity (the token's tokenable).

Rotate the caller's token: revoke the one presented and issue a fresh
ability-scoped replacement. Sanctum tokens don't expire on their own, so
rotation is how a client renews a credential it wants to cycle.
Revoke the token presented with the request (single-device logout).
Admin authentication
item.description:
Token auth for the `admin` (staff) guard: login/logout/refresh/me plus
provisioning of new staff. Admin tokens carry the `admin` ability AND the
admin's granular spatie permissions (manage-flows, moderate-content, …) as
abilities, so future admin API routes can gate with `abilities:<permission>`.
There is deliberately no public admin sign-up — staff are provisioned by an
existing admin holding `manage-users`.

item.name: Authenticate credentials and issue an ability-scoped token.

request.description:
Throttled by the route middleware; credentials are verified against the
entity model (constant-time hash check) with a single generic error to
avoid leaking which half was wrong.

item.name: Return the authenticated entity (the token's tokenable).

Rotate the caller's token: revoke the one presented and issue a fresh
ability-scoped replacement. Sanctum tokens don't expire on their own, so
rotation is how a client renews a credential it wants to cycle.
Revoke the token presented with the request (single-device logout).

request.description:
Create a new staff account with optional roles. Restricted to an
authenticated admin holding `manage-users`; audited with the acting admin
as causer. Returns the created admin (no token — the new admin logs in
themselves).

item.name: Discovery
item.description:
Public, read-only talent discovery for the mobile app plus the two public
write actions a visitor can take on a profile (leave a review, send a booking
enquiry) — the same query, services and validation the web pages use.
Published talents only.

query.description: view_count or created_at (prefix with - for descending).

request.description:
Paginated, filterable discovery feed. Supply `filter[...]` params
(type, category, availability, city, country, equipment, software, q) and
`sort` (view_count, created_at) exactly as the web feed does.

request.description:
The full public passport for one published talent, resolved by slug: the
visible profile blocks, profession(s), comp card, rate-card services and
approved reviews. Translatable fields come back in the request locale
(Accept-Language). Bumps the profile view counter.

One published talent's project (case study), resolved by id and scoped to
the talent in the path (404 otherwise).

A past client leaves a review on a published talent; it lands pending for
the talent to moderate.

The public booking CTA — lands in `deal_enquiries` (availability-checked)
and converts to a deal once a brand picks it up.

Paginated, filterable public brand directory (published brands only).
Whitelisted filters and sorts mirror the web search contract.

The public brand profile, resolved by slug: credibility, aesthetic, social
handles, image gallery, approved reviews and public campaigns. `description`
comes back in the request locale (Accept-Language). 404 if unpublished.

One public campaign under a published brand, resolved by slug and scoped to
the brand in the path (404 otherwise).

item.name: Deals
item.description:
The authenticated party's view of the shared deal engine. A talent token sees
the deals it is party to; a brand token sees the deals it initiated/received.
Read-only here — step actions (advance/reject/message) stay on the web deal
room for now and are a later API slice.

request.description:
Paginated deals scoped to the authenticated talent or brand, newest
activity first, with the counterparty, service and current step loaded.
A single deal the caller is a party to (403 otherwise).
Pending queues, deals awaiting admin, and flow/catalog counts.

query.description: Filter by log name (e.g. deal_flow, moderation).

request.description: Paginated, newest first, with `deals_count`.
The campaign (roles + gallery) and the deals running under it.
Multipart `file`. Returns the created media row + its URL.

Brand-initiated ("Start a deal"): creates a deal with the resolved active
flow, snapshots its steps, activates the first step and notifies the talent.
Guard failures (talent unpublished / not bookable, brand incomplete, no
active flow) return **422**. Optionally links a campaign (`deals.campaign_id`).

query.description: Filter by deal status (e.g. awaiting_brand, completed).

request.description: Paginated inbox, newest first, with the counterparty + current step loaded.

The deal, its stepper, the message timeline and whether the brand can act
on the current step. Reading the room marks the talent's messages read.

Submits the current step for the brand (submit brief / accept quote / sign /
pay). The body shape depends on the step's `step_type`.

item.name: Post a message to the deal thread

request.description:
Paginated, personalised talents. Ad-hoc narrowing via `filter[...]`
(availability, city, type) and `sort` (view_count, created_at).

item.name: Send a brief to a talent

request.description:
Paginated pre-auth enquiries addressed to the brand's email that are still
awaiting conversion (`status = new`).

item.name: Convert an enquiry to a deal

request.description:
Turns a matched pending enquiry into a real deal (carrying its talent /
service / brief), marks it converted, and notifies the talent. **403** if
the enquiry's email isn't the brand's; **422** if already handled.

Whether onboarding is complete plus the current saved values, so the app
can resume the wizard.
Flips `is_complete` and unlocks the dashboard/feed.
Multipart `file`. Returns the resolved logo URL.
Multipart `file`. Returns the resolved cover URL.
References + mood tags. Reuses the transactional onboarding service.
Multipart `file`. Returns the created row + its URL.
Paginated, approved-only, newest first.
Paginated, newest first.
The number of unread notifications (for a badge).

item.name: Reference / lookups
item.description:
Public catalog data the mobile app needs to render dynamic UI (dropdowns,
profession pickers, the block catalog, the deal flows on offer). Read-only and
unauthenticated — onboarding forms need these before a token exists.
Translatable names come back in the request locale (Accept-Language).

request.description: The full profession catalog.

response.body: {"success":true,"data":[{"id":1,"slug":"model","name":"Model","category":"model","icon":"lucide-model","description":"Model on Fama."},{"id":2,"slug":"photographer","name":"Photographer","category":"crew","icon":"lucide-photographer","description":"Photographer on Fama."},{"id":3,"slug":"cinematographer","name":"Cinematographer (DOP)","category":"crew","icon":"lucide-cinematographer","description":"Cinematographer (DOP) on Fama."},{"id":4,"slug":"creative-director","name":"Creative Director","category":"creative","icon":"lucide-creative-director","description":"Creative Director on Fama."},{"id":5,"slug":"stylist","name":"Stylist","category":"creative","icon":"lucide-stylist","description":"Stylist on Fama."},{"id":6,"slug":"graphic-designer","name":"Graphic Designer","category":"creative","icon":"lucide-graphic-designer","description":"Graphic Designer on Fama."}],"message":null,"errors":null,"meta":null}

request.description: The active profile-block catalog (what a talent can add + how it renders).

The active deal flows on offer, each with its ordered steps so the app can
preview the deal loop before starting one.

request.description:
The enum option lists (brand + talent) that back the app's select inputs, so
forms stay in sync with server-side validation.
Paginated rows for `{type}`, in display order.

item.name: Upload media for a content item

request.description: Multipart `file` (image/video). Returns the item with its conversion URL.

query.description: Filter by deal status (e.g. awaiting_talent, completed).

request.description: Paginated inbox, newest first, with the counterparty + current step loaded.

The deal, its stepper, the message timeline and whether the talent can act
on the current step. Reading the room marks the brand's messages read.

Submits the current step for the talent (send quote / accept / upload / sign
/ pay). The body shape depends on the step's `step_type`.

item.name: Post a message to the deal thread

request.description: Paginated, newest first.
The linked (ordered) professions and the ones still available to add.

Core fields (translatables as per-locale maps), linked professions and the
ordered profile blocks.
Multipart `image`. Returns the resolved hero URL (medialibrary).

The block types still available to add, filtered by the talent's profession
eligibility.

query.description: Filter by pending, approved or rejected.
